creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Walter Schwarzenfeld from comment #9)

In (for example):

fprintf(stderr, "File '%s', size: %lld\n", name, (unsigned long long)size);

%lld gives a signed interpretation vs. the (unsigned long long) cast.

The compiler reported long long in all 3 cases. At least for the one
that was off_t (aka long long), signed is appropriate and unsigned
would not be. For ILP32 off_t on linux (long long) would sign extend
but (unsigned long long) would not, messing up display of negative
offsets.

%llu would give an unsigned interpretation in decimal in the format if
it is needed someplace. (But it may not be needed.)
